revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[User:Immanuelle/sandbox]] to [[User:Kotohira Faith]]
[[WP:AES|←]]Changed redirect target from [[User:Kotohira Faith]] to [[Template:Kotohira Faith]]
[[WP:AES|←]]Removed redirect to [[Template:Kotohira Faith]]
revision.text:
{{Page numbers needed|date=August 2021}}
'''Amenohoakari''' (天火明命, Amenohoakari-no-mikoto) is a god of [[Solar deity|sun]] and [[God of agriculture|agriculture]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. His descendants are called ''Tenson-zoku''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242}}</ref>
== Name == Names for Amenohoakari are listed below.
* Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakarikushitamanigihayahi-no-mikoto (天照国照彦天火明櫛玉饒速日尊) in ''[[Kujiki]]'' * Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakari-no-mikoto (天照國照彦天火明尊) * Amaterukuniteruhikohoakari-no-mikoto (天照国照彦火明命) in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' * Amenohoakari-no-mikoto (天火明命) in ''[[Kojiki]]'' * Hoakari-no-mikoto (火明命) in ''Nihon Shoki'' * Ikishiniho-no-mikoto (膽杵磯丹杵穂命) * Amaterumitama-no-kami (天照御魂神) in ''Jinjyashiryō''
== Genealogy == According to ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Amenohoakari was born to [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] and [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi's]] daughter, [[Yorozuhatahime]]. [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] is his younger brother, but in another book of ''Nihon Shoki'' Ninigi is his father.
In ''[[Kujiki]]'', Amenohoakari is considered the same ''kami'' as [[Nigihayahi]], the ancestral god of the Hozumi and [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] clans, but this contradicts their generational relationships and areas of activity.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref>
In ''Harima-no-kuni Fudoki'', Amenohoakari is the child of [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Dotsuhime]].<ref>{{Cite book|last=Okimori|first=Takuya|title=Harima-no-kuni Fudoki|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2005|isbn=4634593912|location=Japan}}</ref>
== Descendants == In ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the descendants of [[Amatsuhikone]], [[Ame-no-hohi]], and [[Amanomichine]], together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as ''Tenson-zoku''. The ''Tenson-zoku'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High [[Heaven]]) to [[Owari Province|Owari]] and [[Tanba Province|Tanba]] provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of [[Owari clan|Owari]], [[Tsumori clan|Tsumori]], [[Amabe clan|Amabe]], and [[Tanba clan|Tanba]] clans.<ref name=":0" />
However, it is clear that ''Amabe-shi Keizu,'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Kokuho「Amabe-shi Keizu」he no gimon, Kokigi no Heya (国宝「海部氏系図」への疑問 古樹紀之房間)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> and that these clans actually descended from the [[List of water deities|sea deity]] [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, [[Takakuraji]], as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>
== Name meaning == As Amenohoakari's name suggests, he is the deification of sunlight and heat. In ''[[Kojiki-den]]'', Hoakari is written "穂赤熟", which means that the ears of rice ripen and become red. Like the other gods connected to the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]], Amenohoakari's name is also related to rice and is worshiped as the sun god and the god of agriculture.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Motoori|first=Norinaga|title=Kojiki-den|publisher=Nabu Press|year=2011|isbn=978-1178814958}}</ref>
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

{{Page numbers needed|date=August 2021}}
== Name == Names for Amenohoakari are listed below.
* Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakarikushitamanigihayahi-no-mikoto (天照国照彦天火明櫛玉饒速日尊) in ''[[Kujiki]]'' * Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakari-no-mikoto (天照國照彦天火明尊) * Amaterukuniteruhikohoakari-no-mikoto (天照国照彦火明命) in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' * Amenohoakari-no-mikoto (天火明命) in ''[[Kojiki]]'' * Hoakari-no-mikoto (火明命) in ''Nihon Shoki'' * Ikishiniho-no-mikoto (膽杵磯丹杵穂命) * Amaterumitama-no-kami (天照御魂神) in ''Jinjyashiryō''
== Genealogy == According to ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Amenohoakari was born to [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] and [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi's]] daughter, [[Yorozuhatahime]]. [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] is his younger brother, but in another book of ''Nihon Shoki'' Ninigi is his father.
In ''[[Kujiki]]'', Amenohoakari is considered the same ''kami'' as [[Nigihayahi]], the ancestral god of the Hozumi and [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] clans, but this contradicts their generational relationships and areas of activity.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref>
In ''Harima-no-kuni Fudoki'', Amenohoakari is the child of [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Dotsuhime]].<ref>{{Cite book|last=Okimori|first=Takuya|title=Harima-no-kuni Fudoki|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2005|isbn=4634593912|location=Japan}}</ref>
== Descendants == In ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the descendants of [[Amatsuhikone]], [[Ame-no-hohi]], and [[Amanomichine]], together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as ''Tenson-zoku''. The ''Tenson-zoku'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High [[Heaven]]) to [[Owari Province|Owari]] and [[Tanba Province|Tanba]] provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of [[Owari clan|Owari]], [[Tsumori clan|Tsumori]], [[Amabe clan|Amabe]], and [[Tanba clan|Tanba]] clans.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |last=Hanawa |first=Hokiichi |url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242 |title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄) |date=1983 |publisher=Onkogakkai |location=Japan |oclc=959773242}}</ref>
However, it is clear that ''Amabe-shi Keizu,'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Kokuho「Amabe-shi Keizu」he no gimon, Kokigi no Heya (国宝「海部氏系図」への疑問 古樹紀之房間)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> and that these clans actually descended from the [[List of water deities|sea deity]] [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, [[Takakuraji]], as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>
== Name meaning == As Amenohoakari's name suggests, he is the deification of sunlight and heat. In ''[[Kojiki-den]]'', Hoakari is written "穂赤熟", which means that the ears of rice ripen and become red. Like the other gods connected to the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]], Amenohoakari's name is also related to rice and is worshiped as the sun god and the god of agriculture.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Motoori|first=Norinaga|title=Kojiki-den|publisher=Nabu Press|year=2011|isbn=978-1178814958}}</ref>
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: Replaced [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|VE ref names]] using [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer|RefRenamer]]
revision.text:
{{Page numbers needed|date=August 2021}}
== Name == Names for Amenohoakari are listed below.
* Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakarikushitamanigihayahi-no-mikoto (天照国照彦天火明櫛玉饒速日尊) in ''[[Kujiki]]'' * Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakari-no-mikoto (天照國照彦天火明尊) * Amaterukuniteruhikohoakari-no-mikoto (天照国照彦火明命) in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' * Amenohoakari-no-mikoto (天火明命) in ''[[Kojiki]]'' * Hoakari-no-mikoto (火明命) in ''Nihon Shoki'' * Ikishiniho-no-mikoto (膽杵磯丹杵穂命) * Amaterumitama-no-kami (天照御魂神) in ''Jinjyashiryō''
== Genealogy == According to ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Amenohoakari was born to [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] and [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi's]] daughter, [[Yorozuhatahime]]. [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] is his younger brother, but in another book of ''Nihon Shoki'' Ninigi is his father.
In ''[[Kujiki]]'', Amenohoakari is considered the same ''kami'' as [[Nigihayahi]], the ancestral god of the Hozumi and [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] clans, but this contradicts their generational relationships and areas of activity.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref>
In ''Harima-no-kuni Fudoki'', Amenohoakari is the child of [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Dotsuhime]].<ref>{{Cite book|last=Okimori|first=Takuya|title=Harima-no-kuni Fudoki|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2005|isbn=4634593912|location=Japan}}</ref>
== Descendants == In ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the descendants of [[Amatsuhikone]], [[Ame-no-hohi]], and [[Amanomichine]], together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as ''Tenson-zoku''. The ''Tenson-zoku'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High [[Heaven]]) to [[Owari Province|Owari]] and [[Tanba Province|Tanba]] provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of [[Owari clan|Owari]], [[Tsumori clan|Tsumori]], [[Amabe clan|Amabe]], and [[Tanba clan|Tanba]] clans.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hanawa |first=Hokiichi |url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242 |title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄) |date=1983 |publisher=Onkogakkai |location=Japan |oclc=959773242}}</ref>
However, it is clear that ''Amabe-shi Keizu,'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Kokuho「Amabe-shi Keizu」he no gimon, Kokigi no Heya (国宝「海部氏系図」への疑問 古樹紀之房間)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> and that these clans actually descended from the [[List of water deities|sea deity]] [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, [[Takakuraji]], as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>
== Name meaning == As Amenohoakari's name suggests, he is the deification of sunlight and heat. In ''[[Kojiki-den]]'', Hoakari is written "穂赤熟", which means that the ears of rice ripen and become red. Like the other gods connected to the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]], Amenohoakari's name is also related to rice and is worshiped as the sun god and the god of agriculture.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Motoori|first=Norinaga|title=Kojiki-den|publisher=Nabu Press|year=2011|isbn=978-1178814958}}</ref>
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: /* Descendants */
revision.text:
{{Page numbers needed|date=August 2021}}
== Name == Names for Amenohoakari are listed below.
* Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakarikushitamanigihayahi-no-mikoto (天照国照彦天火明櫛玉饒速日尊) in ''[[Kujiki]]'' * Amaterukuniteruhikoamenohoakari-no-mikoto (天照國照彦天火明尊) * Amaterukuniteruhikohoakari-no-mikoto (天照国照彦火明命) in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' * Amenohoakari-no-mikoto (天火明命) in ''[[Kojiki]]'' * Hoakari-no-mikoto (火明命) in ''Nihon Shoki'' * Ikishiniho-no-mikoto (膽杵磯丹杵穂命) * Amaterumitama-no-kami (天照御魂神) in ''Jinjyashiryō''
== Genealogy == According to ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Amenohoakari was born to [[Ame-no-oshihomimi]] and [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi's]] daughter, [[Yorozuhatahime]]. [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] is his younger brother, but in another book of ''Nihon Shoki'' Ninigi is his father.
In ''[[Kujiki]]'', Amenohoakari is considered the same ''kami'' as [[Nigihayahi]], the ancestral god of the Hozumi and [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] clans, but this contradicts their generational relationships and areas of activity.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref>
In ''Harima-no-kuni Fudoki'', Amenohoakari is the child of [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Dotsuhime]].<ref>{{Cite book|last=Okimori|first=Takuya|title=Harima-no-kuni Fudoki|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2005|isbn=4634593912|location=Japan}}</ref>
== Descendants == {{Quote| In ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the descendants of [[Amatsuhikone]], [[Ame-no-hohi]], and [[Amanomichine]], together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as ''Tenson-zoku''. The ''Tenson-zoku'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High [[Heaven]]) to [[Owari Province|Owari]] and [[Tanba Province|Tanba]] provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of [[Owari clan|Owari]], [[Tsumori clan|Tsumori]], [[Amabe clan|Amabe]], and [[Tanba clan|Tanba]] clans.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hanawa |first=Hokiichi |url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242 |title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄) |date=1983 |publisher=Onkogakkai |location=Japan |oclc=959773242}}</ref>
However, it is clear that ''Amabe-shi Keizu,'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Kokuho「Amabe-shi Keizu」he no gimon, Kokigi no Heya (国宝「海部氏系図」への疑問 古樹紀之房間)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> and that these clans actually descended from the [[List of water deities|sea deity]] [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, [[Takakuraji]], as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>|[[User:太西|太西]]}
== Name meaning == As Amenohoakari's name suggests, he is the deification of sunlight and heat. In ''[[Kojiki-den]]'', Hoakari is written "穂赤熟", which means that the ears of rice ripen and become red. Like the other gods connected to the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]], Amenohoakari's name is also related to rice and is worshiped as the sun god and the god of agriculture.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Motoori|first=Norinaga|title=Kojiki-den|publisher=Nabu Press|year=2011|isbn=978-1178814958}}</ref>
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: /* Descendants */
revision.text:
== Descendants == {{Quote| In ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the descendants of [[Amatsuhikone]], [[Ame-no-hohi]], and [[Amanomichine]], together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as ''Tenson-zoku''. The ''Tenson-zoku'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High [[Heaven]]) to [[Owari Province|Owari]] and [[Tanba Province|Tanba]] provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of [[Owari clan|Owari]], [[Tsumori clan|Tsumori]], [[Amabe clan|Amabe]], and [[Tanba clan|Tanba]] clans.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Hanawa |first=Hokiichi |url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242 |title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄) |date=1983 |publisher=Onkogakkai |location=Japan |oclc=959773242}}</ref>
However, it is clear that ''Amabe-shi Keizu,'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document,<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Kokuho「Amabe-shi Keizu」he no gimon, Kokigi no Heya (国宝「海部氏系図」への疑問 古樹紀之房間)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> and that these clans actually descended from the [[List of water deities|sea deity]] [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, [[Takakuraji]], as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>|[[User:太西|太西]]}<ref>{{Citation |title=Amenohoakari |date=2021-03-24 |url=https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Amenohoakari&oldid=1014043668 |work=Wikipedia |access-date=2023-12-05 |language=en}}</ref>
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: /* Descendants */

revision.text:
== Deities == Two main gods are important here. The sea god [[Watatsumi]], and his son, Hotakami no Mikoto ({{ill|Utsushihikanasaku|ja|宇都志日金拆命|draft}}) the tutelary deity of the [[Azumi people]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Explore Azumino! - Hotaka Shrine |url=https://azumino-e-tabi.net/en/sightseeing/hotaka-shrine |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=Explore Azumino! |publisher=[[Japan Tourism Agency]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":2">https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001562761.pdf</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-10-01 |title=Mt. Hotaka also have deities enshrined, and these deities are as their tutelaries : JINJA-GAKU 3 {{!}} HIKES IN JAPAN |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001062231/https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=web.archive.org}}</ref> and their ancestor.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ofune Matsuri – A Unique Festival in Nagano, Japan! - Festivals & Events｜COOL JAPAN VIDEOS｜A Website With Information About Travel, Culture, Food, History, and Things to Do in Japan |url=https://cooljapan-videos.com/en/articles/au3wf7b2 |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=cooljapan-videos.com |language=en}}</ref> Hotakami no Mikoto is said to have descended to earth on the nearby [[Mount Hotakadake]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kamikochi - A Place of Special Importance |url=https://www.snowmonkeyresorts.com/activities/kamikochi/a-place-of-special-importance |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=SNOW MONKEY RESORTS}}</ref>{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: /* Deities */

revision.text:
== Deities == Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Explore Azumino! - Hotaka Shrine |url=https://azumino-e-tabi.net/en/sightseeing/hotaka-shrine |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=Explore Azumino! |publisher=[[Japan Tourism Agency]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":2">https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001562761.pdf</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web |date=2020-10-01 |title=Mt. Hotaka also have deities enshrined, and these deities are as their tutelaries : JINJA-GAKU 3 {{!}} HIKES IN JAPAN |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001062231/https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=web.archive.org}}</ref>
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web |title=Ofune Matsuri – A Unique Festival in Nagano, Japan! - Festivals & Events｜COOL JAPAN VIDEOS｜A Website With Information About Travel, Culture, Food, History, and Things to Do in Japan |url=https://cooljapan-videos.com/en/articles/au3wf7b2 |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=cooljapan-videos.com |language=en}}</ref>
Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":1" />
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name=":3" />{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: Alter: url. URLs might have been anonymized. Add: archive-date, archive-url. Removed parameters. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this bot]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | Suggested by Immanuelle | #UCB_toolbar
revision.text:
== Deities == Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Explore Azumino! - Hotaka Shrine |url=https://azumino-e-tabi.net/en/sightseeing/hotaka-shrine |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=Explore Azumino! |publisher=[[Japan Tourism Agency]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":2">https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001562761.pdf</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web |date=2020-10-01 |title=Mt. Hotaka also have deities enshrined, and these deities are as their tutelaries : JINJA-GAKU 3 {{!}} HIKES IN JAPAN |url=https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |access-date=2023-12-06 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001062231/https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |archive-date=2020-10-01 }}</ref>
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web |title=Ofune Matsuri – A Unique Festival in Nagano, Japan! - Festivals & Events｜COOL JAPAN VIDEOS｜A Website With Information About Travel, Culture, Food, History, and Things to Do in Japan |url=https://cooljapan-videos.com/en/articles/au3wf7b2 |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=cooljapan-videos.com |language=en}}</ref>
Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":1" />
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name=":3" />{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: Replaced [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|VE ref names]] using [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer|RefRenamer]]
revision.text:
== Deities == Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name="explore-azumino-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Explore Azumino! - Hotaka Shrine |url=https://azumino-e-tabi.net/en/sightseeing/hotaka-shrine |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=Explore Azumino! |publisher=[[Japan Tourism Agency]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="https-2">https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001562761.pdf</ref><ref name="mt-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-10-01 |title=Mt. Hotaka also have deities enshrined, and these deities are as their tutelaries : JINJA-GAKU 3 {{!}} HIKES IN JAPAN |url=https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |access-date=2023-12-06 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001062231/https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |archive-date=2020-10-01 }}</ref>
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name="cooljapanvideoscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Ofune Matsuri – A Unique Festival in Nagano, Japan! - Festivals & Events｜COOL JAPAN VIDEOS｜A Website With Information About Travel, Culture, Food, History, and Things to Do in Japan |url=https://cooljapan-videos.com/en/articles/au3wf7b2 |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=cooljapan-videos.com |language=en}}</ref>
Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name="explore-azumino-2023a" /><ref name="https-2" /><ref name="mt-2020a" />
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name="cooljapanvideoscom-2023a" />{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== References == <references /> [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Solar gods]]

revision.comment: /* References */Changed article categories to category links per [[WP:USERNOCAT]]
revision.text:
== Deities == Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name="explore-azumino-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Explore Azumino! - Hotaka Shrine |url=https://azumino-e-tabi.net/en/sightseeing/hotaka-shrine |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=Explore Azumino! |publisher=[[Japan Tourism Agency]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="https-2">https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001562761.pdf</ref><ref name="mt-2020a">{{Cite web |date=2020-10-01 |title=Mt. Hotaka also have deities enshrined, and these deities are as their tutelaries : JINJA-GAKU 3 {{!}} HIKES IN JAPAN |url=https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |access-date=2023-12-06 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001062231/https://hikesinjapan.yamakei-online.com/topics/jinjagaku03.php |archive-date=2020-10-01 }}</ref>
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name="cooljapanvideoscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Ofune Matsuri – A Unique Festival in Nagano, Japan! - Festivals & Events｜COOL JAPAN VIDEOS｜A Website With Information About Travel, Culture, Food, History, and Things to Do in Japan |url=https://cooljapan-videos.com/en/articles/au3wf7b2 |access-date=2023-12-06 |website=cooljapan-videos.com |language=en}}</ref>
Parentage of Utsushi.<ref name="explore-azumino-2023a" /><ref name="https-2" /><ref name="mt-2020a" />
Ancestor of Azumi people.<ref name="cooljapanvideoscom-2023a" />{{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== References == <references /> [[:Category:Japanese gods]] [[:Category:Shinto kami]] [[:Category:Solar gods]]

{{Navbox | name = Sarutahiko Faith | title = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Faith]] | listclass = hlist | image = [[File:Sarutahiko Ōkami.crop.4.wp.working.print.png|150px|link=|alt=]] |group1 = Main Deities |list1 = * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] * [[Ame-no-Uzume]]
|group2 = Shrines |list2 = * [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine|ja|猿田彦神社|simple}} |group3 = Branch shrines |list3= * [[Shirahige Shrine]] * [[Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]] }}<noinclude>
[[Category:Shinto templates]] [[Category:Shinto cult templates]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[sn:" to "[[Category:sn_crud|TEST"
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Sarutahiko Faith | title = [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko Faith]] | listclass = hlist | image = [[File:Sarutahiko Ōkami crop from Wittig collection.png|150px|link=|alt=]] |group1 = Main Deities |list1 = * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] * [[Ame-no-Uzume]]
|group2 = Shrines |list2 = * [[:en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine|Tsubaki Grand Shrine]] * [[Sarutahiko Shrine]] * [[Koma Shrine]] |group3 = Branch shrines |list3= * [[Shirahige Shrine]] * [[:en:Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America|Tsubaki Grand Shrine of America]] |group4= misc |list4= * {{ill|Sarume clan|ja|猿女君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarume clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarume clan|qq=}} }}<noinclude>
[[Category:sn_crud|TESTSarutahiko Faith]]</noinclude><noinclude>[[en:Template:Sarutahiko Faith]]</noinclude> <noinclude>{{wikidata link|Q124107341}}</noinclude> [[Category:Shinto cult templates]] [[Category:Shinto templates]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23582763848|commit triggered pipeline]]
